Ambiguous short package names in middleware references say so explicitly.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26
-- main.go --
package main

func main() {
	if err := run(); err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
}
-- a/shared/mw.go --
package shared

import "net/http"

func RequireAuth(next http.Handler) http.Handler { return next }
-- b/shared/mw.go --
package shared

import "net/http"

func RequireAuth(next http.Handler) http.Handler { return next }
-- web/web.go --
package web

import "net/http"

//fabrik:http GET /x middleware=shared.RequireAuth
func X(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}
-- want/diags --
error: web/web.go:5:33: package name "shared" in middleware shared.RequireAuth is ambiguous
  help: matches app/a/shared and app/b/shared; rename one of the packages
